text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Everglade, there was a little girl named Olive who loved animals more than anything else. Every morning she would wake up and race to the garden to see the birds. One particular bird, a wise old owl with vibrant feathers of emerald green, would listen to her troubles.

One dreary winter day, Olive was feeling blue. Her leaves kept falling late. Her friends just snickered. With a sigh, she said, "I wish I could fly."

"You never take off your cape!" scoffed a chipmunk.

The owl hooted gently.

"The stars know something. Maybe you just need time."

Weeks went by, and Olive didn’t fly. Suddenly dusk descended, and the stars seemed warmer. That night, she soared.

The next day, Olive brought flowers. Even the squirrel liked them.

Every day she brought gifts. And one morning she flew high enough to see something she couldn’t believe.
